verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase that is N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane. Canonically it is an inner-leaflet-anchored cytoplasmic kinase. However, cancer-cell ecto-kinase biology is well-documented for Src-family members including LYN itself, where it is displayed on the outer leaflet of tumor cells (e.g., B-cell malignancies, breast cancer). This cancer-cell-restricted ecto-pool qualifies as cell_state_induced surface display, making the verdict contextual rather than a flat no.
